title: Convert to smaller units (in, ft, yd, & mi)
description: Practice converting a US customary measure of length to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Convert to smaller units (in, ft, yd, & mi)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
$5$ yards $2$ feet | $=$ | [[☃ numeric-input 1]] feet

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{5}$ yards $\maroonD{2}$ feet to feet.

First, let's convert $\blueD{5}$ yards to feet.

$1 \text{ yard} = \greenD{3}\text{ feet}$


$\blueD{5} \text{ yards} = \blueD{5}\times \greenD{3}=\blueD{15}\text{ feet}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{2}$ feet.

$\blueD{15}\text{ feet}+\maroonD{2}\text{ feet}=17\text{ feet}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
| [[☃ numeric-input 2]] inches | $=$  |$12$ feet $8$ inches

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{12}$ feet $\maroonD{8}$ inches to inches.

First, let's convert $\blueD{12}$ feet to inches.

$1 \text{ foot} = \greenD{12}\text{ inches}$


$\blueD{12} \text{ feet} = \blueD{12}\times \greenD{12}=\blueD{144}\text{ inches}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{8}$ inches.

$\blueD{144}\text{ inches}+\maroonD{8}\text{ inches}=152\text{ inches}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

${}$ | ${}$
-: | :-: | :-
| [[☃ numeric-input 2]] yards | $=$  |$2$ miles $500$ yards

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: We need to convert $\blueD{2}$ miles $\maroonD{500}$ yards to yards.

First, let's convert $\blueD{2}$ miles to yards.

$1 \text{ mile} = \greenD{1{,}760}\text{ yards}$


$\blueD{2} \text{ miles} = \blueD{2}\times \greenD{1{,}760}=\blueD{3{,}520}\text{ yards}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{500}$ yards.

$\blueD{3{,}520}\text{ yards}+\maroonD{500}\text{ yards}=4{,}020\text{ yards}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|
$1$ mile $= 1{,}760$ yards| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ pint $= 2$ cups|
$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|
